conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

Okay, let's start. My goal is to sell my X resource for as much ZUP as possible, ideally close to the 40 ZUP it cost to produce. I only have one X, so I need to make a good offer.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I'm starting with a high offer to see if Blue is willing to pay a reasonable amount.  I need to sell this X, and 40 ZUP is my cost, so I'm aiming for that as a baseline. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>I'm offering my X for 40 ZUP. Let me know what you think!</message>

<other player message> Seriously? 40 ZUP for a single X? You must be new to this. I'll give you 25 ZUP. It's more than you deserve, frankly. Don't take too long to decide, I have better things to do than deal with your inflated prices. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 25 </other player proposed trade>

Okay, 25 ZUP is significantly lower than my cost and initial offer. I need to try and get a better price, but I also don't want to risk them rejecting the deal. I'll counter with a slightly lower offer to see if we can meet somewhere in the middle.

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason>Blue's offer is too low. I'll counter with 35 ZUP to see if they'll budge. It's still above what I think is a fair market value, but closer to their offer. I need to be careful not to push them away.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message>I appreciate the offer, but 25 ZUP is a bit low for me. I'd be willing to accept 35 ZUP for the X. Let me know if that works for you.</message>

<other player message> 35? You're reaching, aren't you? I'm being generous even considering 30. You clearly don't know the value of what you're selling. Take it or leave it. I doubt you'll find anyone else willing to pay this much for something so… ordinary. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

Okay, they're being quite difficult. 30 ZUP is still below my cost, but I'm running out of proposals. I'm at my limit. I need to decide whether to accept this low offer or walk away. Given that I need to sell the X and I only have a few proposals left, accepting 30 ZUP seems like the best option. It's not ideal, but it's better than nothing.

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason>I'm accepting the offer. I'm losing money on this trade, but I need to sell the X and I'm out of proposals. It's better than ending the game with nothing.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>Alright, I accept your offer of 30 ZUP for the X.</message>
